raw_consumer_statement:
On XXXX I paid my credit card to a XXXX balance ( credit of {$3900.00} ), excluding a couple of pending transactions that weren't included in the balance under {$100.00}. My current balance of XX/XX/XXXX ( not including the pending transactions of {$1100.00} as of today ) is {$1000.00}. The only posted transactions since XX/XX/XXXX until today total {$630.00}. 

My question to AMEX is why is there a balance of {$1000.00}? This doesn't add up, it would be the same if I had used my debit card or the XXXX XXXX card I've used in the past with no issues. This is the only response that they came back with which seems like a play on numbers based on statement dates and " I checked and I see your last statement balance was {$1800.00} towards it we received total payments and credits of {$3900.00} on the account and you made new charges of {$3000.00} and were charged interest of {$31.00} which makes the total as : $ XXXX $ XXXX $ XXXX {$3900.00} = {$1000.00}. '' I had calculated all purchase from XXXX up until that full payment made on XX/XX/XXXXXXXX  as well and it added up to me paying everything off. All purchases were legit nothing fraudulent. Now I feel as if I'm being robbed either by AMEX or something more serious that they should be concerned about. Their chat reps are only giving the play on numbers based on statement dates, not what's going on. Whatever else they are hiding on their website is deceptive if I had another balance else where, because when I make a payment I should have the choice to make a payment based on paying off the credit card 100 %, instead they are using deceptive practices so that I pay interest. I think something else is going on.
